title: Arthur the King Review - Screen Daily Critic Assessment
text: Film details: Director Simon Cellan Jones' sports drama stars Mark Wahlberg as Michael, a retired adventure racer competing in one final championship in the Dominican Republic. The film is based on Mikael Lindnord's memoir and features Simu Liu, Nathalie Emmanuel, and Ali Suliman. Critic Tim Grierson notes that while the film showcases fascinating aspects of adventure racing, including strategic route-planning and physically demanding sequences, it ultimately struggles with execution. He observes that this sports drama oversells its emotional moments. The narrative falters when a stray dog named Arthur becomes central to the plot. Grierson argues the animal functions primarily as a cuddly narrative device symbolizing Michael's need to abandon competition and embrace family life. Wahlberg delivers his familiar earnest everyman characterization, which Grierson suggests has begun showing diminishing returns. Supporting cast members Liu and Emmanuel bring charm to their roles, though the lead character remains underdeveloped. Despite gorgeous Dominican locations and one standout zipline sequence, the film relies too heavily on manipulation rather than genuine inspiration in its final act, making it predictable rather than innovative.
